rationale: The citing paper uses Van Heerde, Leeflang, and Wittink (2000) in its econometric setup for promotion dynamics, explicitly adopting the lead/lag testing approach (starting with up to six weeks and iteratively removing insignificant lags). It also uses that prior work to interpret expected postpromotion dip behavior for price promotions when positioning its own CMP results (including the finding of no CMP postpromotion dip). This is a functional methodological and interpretive role, not just a background mention.
relation_description: The citing study applies the cited paper's promotion-dynamics logic and lag-selection practice to model CMP and PP effects in store-level sales data. It uses the cited paper as prior evidence on postpromotion dips to benchmark and interpret its own promotion-effect results.
